preFix.title: Selecting an MCS-certified installer for solar PV or heat pump
preFix.subtitle: What MCS accreditation means, what a good quote covers, and how to compare
preFix.excerpt: MCS certification is required for SEG payments on solar PV and BUS grants on heat pumps. But not all MCS-certified companies are equal. This covers what MCS accreditation actually means, what a compliant quote must contain, and the red flags that indicate a low-quality installation.
preFix.sourceNotes: MCS website installer directory and scheme requirements. Which? guidance on solar PV installer selection. NESTA/NEF reporting on heat pump installer quality variation.

content.text: The
MCS

certification mark means the installer has passed a third-party audit of competence and installation standards. It is a minimum threshold, not a quality guarantee. An MCS-certified installer can still do poor work. An uncertified installer cannot register for
SEG
payments or
BUS
grant regardless of work quality.
Confirming MCS status

Search the MCS installer directory at mcscertified.com by postcode and technology. It shows the company name, registration number, and what technologies they are certified for. Confirm the right technology is listed before signing. MCS certificates expire each year.
What a compliant solar PV quote must contain
Predicted annual generation in kWh, calculated using the MCS 012 methodology (or PVGIS with specified inputs). The quote should state the roof azimuth and pitch used.
Panel make, model, rated power (Wp), and efficiency. Inverter make, model, and rated output power. Battery make, model, and usable capacity if a battery is included.
Shading assessment: a formal shading analysis using Solmetric, PV Sol, or equivalent. Not just a visual inspection.
G98 or G99 DNO notification: whether the installer will handle the grid connection notice. This is the installer's job, not the homeowner's.
Commissioning certificate and MCS installation certificate on completion.
Red flags in a solar PV quote
Generation figure quoted without stating the assumptions (roof orientation, pitch, shading losses, system efficiency). A high generation figure from an east-facing 45° pitch is a red flag.

No structural assessment offered for a roof that looks more than 25 years old. Tile hooks for mounting must penetrate the tile and fix to sound battens; an old soft-batten roof may need repairs before installation.
Payment in full required before installation starts. Industry standard is a deposit (10 to 20%) on contract signing, staged payments during installation, and final payment on commissioning.

No scaffolding cost included for a two-storey roof. Safe working at height requires scaffolding; a quote that omits scaffolding either uses alternative edge protection or is priced without adequate safety.
Heat pump installer additional checks

For heat pump installations under the BUS grant, the installer must also hold MCS Heat Pump certification (separate from solar PV). The installer must carry out a heat loss calculation per BS EN 12831 for each heated room, and the proposed heat pump output must match the design heat loss at the design flow temperature. Ask to see the heat loss calculation. A quote with no room-by-room heat loss figure is underspecified; a heat pump sized from the boiler kW rating rather than from a heat loss calculation will be oversized and cycle inefficiently.
When to call a professional
Always use an MCS-certified installer for solar PV, batteries, EV charging, and heat pumps under BUS. MCS certification is required for grant and SEG eligibility.
